description: Fix backend wiring: propagate backend selection and API keys from GUI settings into the Phase 3 translation worker so paid backends (DeepL, Azure, OpenAI) are actually used instead of always defaulting to GoogleFreeTranslator. Add backend-availability check in settings and improve error messages when keys are missing or SDKs are not installed.
steps: In src/stx/gui/workers.py TranslationWorker.__init__, add backend_name and api_key parameters (defaulting to 'google' and None)
In TranslationWorker.run(), replace the hard-coded 'translator = GoogleFreeTranslator()' with 'translator = make_backend(self._backend_name, **kwargs)' where kwargs includes api_key if provided
In src/stx/gui/pages/phase3_translate.py _on_start(), read the backend and api_key from gui_settings and pass them to TranslationWorker

In src/stx/gui/settings_dialog.py, add a 'Check availability' button or inline validation that tests whether the selected backend's SDK is importable and whether an API key is configured (either in the field or in the environment variable). Show a clear status label (green/red) next to the backend combo.
In src/stx/translate/factory.py, add a check_backend_available(key) function that returns (available: bool, reason: str) by trying to import the SDK and checking env var / key presence
Improve error messages in deepl_paid.py, azure.py, openai_llm.py __post_init__ to mention both the env var name AND that the key can be set in Edit -> Settings
acceptance_criteria: TranslationWorker no longer hardcodes GoogleFreeTranslator - it reads the backend setting
If backend='deepl' and api_key is set in settings, the DeepLTranslator is constructed
If no api_key and no env var is set for a paid backend, a clear error message surfaces before translation starts
The settings dialog shows availability status for the selected backend
Existing tests still pass (the default remains google so no behavior change for existing users)
findings: All 65 tests pass. check_backend_available correctly reports SDK missing (openai) and key missing (deepl/azure) states. The google backend remains the default so existing behavior is unchanged. The _update_backend_status method in settings_dialog also considers the local API key field text so that users get immediate green status when they type a key.
